source_jobs.title: Senior Marketing Designer
source_jobs.description:
Role overview:

 - As the first senior marketing designer in-house at Dispatch, you own brand execution across every surface.

 - You report to the co-founder and COO and work directly with the founding Product Designer on visual identity, the Head of Marketing on campaigns and content, and the revenue team on sales materials.

 - You build and direct: producing decks, web pages, and campaign assets yourself while setting the creative standard for contractors and other designers.

 - The brand is early. You'll shape it as Dispatch scales into enterprise financial advisory. The pace here is fast; the standard is high.



What you'll own:

 - Brand ownership — Set the visual direction for Dispatch's brand and drive its expression across every surface. You write the guidelines, build the assets, maintain the libraries, direct design contractors, and handle whatever each surface requires to keep the brand consistent.

 - Marketing execution — Execute across the full range of marketing deliverables (conference keynotes, event booths, case study pages, campaign assets, and sales collateral) at craft level and on deadline.

 - Web design — Partner with the Webflow-native production designer on web initiatives. You set the brief and hold the quality bar across pages, components, and flows.



Experience & background:

 - 8+ years of design experience, with senior in-house experience at a company recognized for brand craft.

 - Built a brand system from initial concept through dedicated guidelines and org-wide implementation.

 - Directed junior designers or design contractors across deliverables, with ownership over the brief, the feedback cycle, and the final output quality.

 - Startup experience required: Series B or earlier, or fewer than 100 people at time of joining, ideally as one of the first marketing hires.

 - Presented brand decisions to a strong-opinion founder team and landed the work by changing minds, finding common ground, or both.

 - Webflow-literate at minimum; production fluency preferred.

 - Background in brand system work, not primarily product/UI design, motion, or isolated marketing production.



You'll stand out if you have:

 - B2B or enterprise-facing brand experience (consumer brand background is a plus).

 - Experience at a B2B SaaS company known for strong brand work (Wiz, Gong, etc.).

 - Experience with the latest AI tools and technologies.



Characteristics you have:

 - Brand Craft — Your aesthetic bar is high and internalized. You know what's right before you can explain why, and that sensibility shows in every decision: type, color, layout, and hierarchy chosen with intention, coherent across every surface the brand touches.

 - Sharp Judgment — You make creative decisions with speed and intention, and can articulate why each one is right. When a direction isn't serving the work, you say so and come with an alternative.

 - Audience-Driven — You design for a specific audience, not a generic one. You understand that Dispatch's brand has to earn credibility with enterprise buyers, and your choices reflect that in type, tone, and layout.

 - Startup-Speed Execution — You don't need a lot of structure to produce strong work. You scope your own briefs, fill gaps with judgment, and ship without waiting for everything to align. You thrive with ownership.

 - High Standards — You take feedback seriously and iterate without ego. When work isn't landing, you want to know, and you do the work to get it there. What ships reflects your standards, not just what was asked for.



Things to highlight about Dispatch:

 - First senior marketing designer in-house, with direct access to company founders, Head of Marketing, and founding Product Designer.

 - Data orchestration platform for wealth management: the shared data layer underneath a fragmented stack of custodians (Schwab, Fidelity, Pershing) and advisor tools (Salesforce, Black Diamond, Orion, eMoney, DocuSign, and more).

 - $30M raised, primarily backed

source_jobs.title: Staff Fullstack Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Role overview:

We're looking for a Staff fullstack engineer to set technical direction for Dispatch's platform. You'll own the highest-leverage architecture decisions, ship the systems behind our hardest problems, and force-multiply multiple engineering teams. This is a senior IC role — no direct reports — reporting to an Engineering Manager.

Where you'll make an impact:

 - Lead the architecture for our hardest cross-cutting platform decisions — service boundaries, data models, sync semantics, and integration patterns — and make them defensibly, in writing and in conversation with the engineers who will live with them.

 - Ship the highest-complexity systems end-to-end — data orchestration, sync, custodian integrations, and customer-facing apps. You stay hands-on in production code.

 - Raise the technical bar across the engineering org on system design, code quality, and observability. Engineers across teams seek your review because it sharpens their work.

 - Partner with Product, Design, and engineering leadership to shape the roadmap — surfacing trade-offs, sequencing decisions, and risks the plan depends on.

 - Mentor and uplevel senior engineers without managing them. Your influence shows up in design reviews, the systems you build, and the example you set.

Requirements:

 - 10+ years of engineering experience with a personal track record of architecting and shipping high-scale, high-complexity production systems.

 - Deep fullstack depth — comfortable writing production Node and React at a high level of craft.

 - Experience setting technical direction across multiple engineers or teams — you've been the person whose architectural calls others built on top of.

 - Strong record of working closely with cross-functional product teams to ship customer-facing software that holds up in production.

 - Meaningful time at an early-stage, high-growth company — startup work as a core part of your career, not a brief stint, on teams that shipped weekly or faster with output that held up under that pace.

 - A career spent hands-on in production code, shipping end-to-end — not specs written or systems proposed, but production outcomes you owned: the hardest bugs, gnarliest migrations, and most fragile systems.

 - Evidence of force-multiplying the engineers around you — through design reviews, mentorship, or code that became a template others built from. References should speak to how the bar moved up around you.

You'll stand out if you have:

 - Experience building B2B software and/or fintech software — bonus for wealth management, custody, financial data infrastructure, public APIs, or MCP servers.

 - Experience at seed/early-stage startups (eg, being a first-10 engineer at a company).

 - Experience with distributed systems at scale — event streaming, sync engines, idempotency, consistency models.

 - Experience building and maintaining open source software.

 - Previously started a company and/or launched personal projects.

 - Experience with AI development tooling, or building AI/LLM features into production products.

Our tech stack:

React, Node, Fastify, MUI, React Query, Vite, Storybook, Playwright, Go, Postgres, gRPC, Protobuf, Redis, AWS, Terraform, GitHub Actions

Dispatch provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local laws.

source_jobs.title: Enterprise Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
About Us:

Dispatch helps financial advisors manage their client data. We are the first data orchestration company for the $80T advisory market – we collect, sync and maintain data across the advisory tech stack. Our platform orchestrates complex data workflows like automated onboarding, account opening and data syncing. We give advisors the power of an integrated tech stack with the choice to use whatever tools are best to run their business. We’re an experienced team of start-up operators with deep roots in both tech and industry. We've raised $30M+ from top-tier investors. You can learn more about us at www.dispatch.io.




THE ROLE

The Enterprise Account Executive will own the full sales cycle — from pipeline creation to close — for prospects within the financial services and wealth management markets. You’ll partner closely with our customer success organization to land and expand strategic accounts.

This role is for an experienced AE who can operate with autonomy, build repeatable processes, and close complex, multi-stakeholder enterprise deals.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Drive the full sales process from prospecting through negotiation and close for enterprise accounts.

 - Build and manage a strong pipeline of opportunities within your assigned territory or vertical.

 - Qualify rigorously using MEDDPICC/MEDDIC methodology

 - Develop account strategies that map buying centers, identify champions, and guide prospects through a complex evaluation.

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with Solutions, Product, and Customer Success to ensure seamless implementation and expansion.

 - Contribute to sales process improvements, messaging refinement, and go-to-market strategy as an early member of the revenue team.

 - Represent Dispatch with professionalism and precision in every customer interaction.


REQUIREMENTS

 - 5+ years of Enterprise AE experience in B2B SaaS.

 - Proven record of exceeding quota and earning top-performer recognition.

 - Preferred: Experience selling products that move, transform, or orchestrate data (e.g., integrations, APIs, iPaaS, data platforms).

 - Startup experience required — joined a company no later than Series B or with fewer than 100 employees.

 - MEDDPICC or MEDDIC trained; disciplined in qualification and forecasting.

 - Exceptional written and verbal communication skills; comfortable selling to senior executives and technical stakeholders.

 - Bachelor’s degree required


YOU ARE:

 - Smart: Quick to learn, able to process complexity, and confident making judgment calls that advance deals.

 - Execution Focused: Runs tight, strategic sales cycles with precision and urgency.

 - Polished: Communicates with executive presence, clarity, and confidence.

 - Entrepreneurial: Thrives in ambiguity, builds structure where none exists, and takes ownership end-to-end.


WHY DISPATCH

 - Ground-floor opportunity at a high-growth SaaS company backed by leading investors.

 - Direct access to the leadership team and influence on GTM strategy.

 - Competitive compensation with equity upside.

 - A culture of excellence, trust, and speed — where great people do the best work of their careers.

Dispatch provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local laws.

source_jobs.title: Backend Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

The financial advisory industry is a $80T market in the US, but has seen little technological innovation compared to the self-service market (Robinhood, Betterment, Wealthfront, SoFi). Client data management is a universally painful and expensive problem for financial advisors. Today, advisors manually collect, update, and utilize client data across the 7-10 software tools that they use to run their business. Data transcription and translation across systems is time-consuming and error-prone, leading to costly inefficiencies and embarrassing client-facing problems. Managing data access control, audit logs, and compliance is a constant challenge, particularly in larger organizations.

We are building the first wealth tech data orchestration company. We sync client data across a financial advisor's tech stack, unlocking the efficiency of a fully-integrated software suite with the choice of tools that best fit their business. We are laser-focused on enabling the best technology experiences for advisors and unlocking the potential of the entire wealth tech software ecosystem. We’re an experienced team of start-up operators with deep roots in both tech and advisory. You can learn more about us on our website https://www.oneadvisory.co/.




ROLE DETAILS


ROLE OVERVIEW:

We're looking for a backend engineer to help build and scale our integration infrastructure. You'll work closely with our staff backend engineer to tackle complex problems in data mapping, syncing, and platform reliability while expanding our integration capabilities across the wealth tech ecosystem.




WHERE YOU'LL MAKE AN IMPACT:

 - Build and maintain integrations with third-party financial systems, expanding our platform's connectivity across the wealth tech landscape.

 - Solve complex data mapping and synchronization challenges, ensuring accurate and reliable data flow between disparate systems with different schemas and constraints.

 - Develop platform capabilities that enable seamless, scalable data orchestration for our customers' tech stacks.

 - Own end-to-end delivery of backend features and services, from design through deployment and monitoring.

 - Contribute to our integration infrastructure, building reusable patterns and tooling that make adding new integrations faster and more reliable.

 - Collaborate with frontend, product, and design teams to deliver high-quality customer experiences backed by robust backend systems.




REQUIREMENTS:

 - 4-6 years of backend engineering experience with a track record of shipping reliable, well-tested systems.

 - Strong fundamentals in backend development, including API design, data modeling, concurrent systems, and performance optimization.

 - Experience working with relational databases and distributed systems concepts.

 - Curious and eager to learn - you're excited to dig into unfamiliar systems, understand complex integrations, and grow your technical skills.

 - Bias toward action and ownership - you see a problem, propose solutions, and drive it to completion without needing constant direction.

 - Strong communication skills and comfort working cross-functionally with product, design, and other engineers.

 - Thrive in fast-paced environments with evolving priorities and high autonomy.

 - Interest and experience with AI tools and prompting - you leverage AI in your workflow and can help level up the team's capabilities.




YOU'LL STAND OUT IF YOU HAVE:

 - Experience building integrations, ETL pipelines, or working with third-party APIs at scale.

 - Exposure to financial services, fintech, or B2B SaaS products.

 - Experience at seed/early-stage startups where you wore multiple hats and moved quickly.

 - Familiarity with event-driven architectures, message queues, or data synchronization patterns.

 - Previously built side projects or contributed to open source software.

Our tech stack: Golang, Redis, PostgreSQL, AWS SQS, Amazon S3




WHY DISPATCH?

Why work at Dispatch?

 - We’re a

source_jobs.title: Senior Engineering Manager
source_jobs.description:
Role overview:

 - Lead one of Dispatch's product engineering teams — the team responsible for our core user workflow and data orchestration platform.

 - Own engineering execution end to end: roadmap sequencing with Product, project scoping, timeline accountability, and cross-functional progress communication.

 - Serve as the connective tissue between Product and Engineering — ensuring the team builds the right things in the right order, at the pace the business demands.

 - Drive engineering hiring end-to-end: headcount planning, JDs, interview loops, and closing.



What you'll own:

 - Team Building & Development — Hire, onboard, and develop a growing team of engineers. Create a culture of high standards, ownership, and craft — making Dispatch an exceptional place to build.

 - Engineering Execution — Own the delivery system: project scoping, milestone planning, timeline accountability, and stakeholder communication. Ensure the team ships on time, with consistency and quality, across the core product, integrations, and platform.

 - Engineering Acceleration — Partner with staff engineers and tech leads who own technical decisions and architecture. Your job is to remove friction, raise the floor, and identify opportunities to accelerate the team through better tooling and automation.

 - Cross-Functional Operating Model — Work closely with Product and Design to triage, prioritize, and sequence work. Serve as the escalation point for customer-impacting engineering issues and coordinate resolution with CS and Product.

 - Incident & Production Management — Own the team's on-call posture, incident response process, and post-mortems. Drive improvements that reduce recurrence and increase system reliability.



Experience & Background:

 - 10+ years in software engineering, including meaningful time as an IC and as an Engineering Manager.
   
   - 5+ years as an individual contributor engineer, with a track record of shipping high-quality, high-complexity systems
   
   - 5+ years managing engineering teams that have done the same

 - Proven track record of growing and scaling an engineering organization — ideally from a small team to 20–30+ engineers.

 - Experience at an early-stage startup — Series B or earlier, or fewer than 100 people at time of joining.



Characteristics:

 - Player-Coach — you don't manage from a distance. You're in the weeds when it matters: unblocking hard problems, running tight team processes, and setting the standard through your own work. You lead by doing, not just directing.

 - High-Output Operator — you know how to get the most out of a team. You bring the right combination of coaching, accountability, and hands-on support to build a group that moves fast, executes with quality, and keeps raising the bar.

 - Systems Builder — you create just enough structure to keep a fast-moving team aligned — without letting process become overhead. You build what's needed to hire well, run cleanly, and ship consistently, and you know when to stop.

 - People Developer — you're genuinely invested in making engineers better. You coach actively, give direct feedback, and create an environment where people grow fast and do their best work.

 - Strong Cross-Functional Operator — you work fluidly with Product and GTM, translate between technical and business contexts, and hold the team accountable to outcomes that matter to customers — not just engineering metrics.



You'll stand out if you have:

 - Experience at seed/early-stage startups — being an early engineer or engineering manager during rapid growth phases

 - Experience with:
   
   - Building and shipping software for enterprise customers — understanding the complexity of long sales cycles, multi-stakeholder implementations, and the reliability expectations that come with it.
   
   - Building with leading fintech APIs (Plaid, Stripe, etc).
   
   - Building APIs-as-a-product and/or B2B products.
   
   - Building data-intensive B2B platforms — partic

source_jobs.title: Senior Fullstack Engineer

ABOUT US



The financial advisory industry is a $80T market in the US, but has seen little technological innovation compared to the self-service market (Robinhood, Betterment, Wealthfront, SoFi). Client data management is a universally painful and expensive problem for financial advisors. Today, advisors manually collect, update, and utilize client data across the 7-10 software tools that they use to run their business. Data transcription and translation across systems is time-consuming and error-prone, leading to costly inefficiencies and embarrassing client-facing problems. Managing data access control, audit logs, and compliance is a constant challenge, particularly in larger organizations.



We are building the first wealth tech data orchestration company. We sync client data across a financial advisor's tech stack, unlocking the efficiency of a fully-integrated software suite with the choice of tools that best fit their business. We are laser-focused on enabling the best technology experiences for advisors and unlocking the potential of the entire wealth tech software ecosystem. We’re an experienced team of start-up operators with deep roots in both tech and advisory. You can learn more about us on our website https://www.oneadvisory.co/.




ROLE DETAILS



Role overview:

We’re looking for an experienced fullstack engineer who can lead the development of our early product. You'll help evolve our engineering culture, take end-to-end ownership over the features that you build, and heavily influence the product roadmap and architecture decisions.



Where you'll make an impact:

 - Lead areas of development critical to the business, building features such as: self-service onboarding, data blending and deconfliction, and digital account opening.

 - As an early employee, you'll have a large role to play in shaping the company's technical vision and product roadmap.

 - Help us build and maintain a strong (and enjoyable!) developer experience.

 - Ensure that the product operates smoothly in production, leveraging observability and monitoring tools to triage and resolve operational issues.

 - Help hire and mentor other engineers as we build out our fast-growing team.



Requirements:

 - Strong track record of shipping high-quality systems with high complexity and scale.

 - Experience building high-quality, delightful user-facing web applications.

 - Experience working closely with a cross-functional product team.

 - Excited to primarily be an individual contributor with a ton of ownership.

 - You thrive in an early-stage, fast-paced environment with a high degree of ambiguity and agency.

 - Impact-driven — you focus on the most important problems to solve and execute to deliver. You get shit done.

 - Bias towards action and ownership – when you see a problem, you fix it.

 - Customer-centric – you are focused on creating the best customer experience possible.

 - Move fast — you can operate at breakneck speed and work furiously to ship, ship, ship.

 - 7+ years of engineering experience.



You’ll stand out if you have:

 - Experience building B2B software and/or fintech software.

 - Experience at seed/early-stage startups (eg, being a first-3 engineer at a company).

 - Experience building and maintaining open source software.

 - Previously started a company and/or launched personal projects.

 - Experience with AI promting, models, and tools. You are someone who not only leverages these in your work, but can help level-up our team.



Our tech stack:

React, Node, Fastify, MUI, React Query, Vite, Storybook, Playwright

Why work at Dispatch?

 - We’re a highly experienced team with a track record of successfully building together. We’re well-versed in building for the best tech companies and asset managers.

 - We’re a well-funded company backed by top-tier investors.

 - We're laser-focused on customers and believe that a world-class user experience is critical for product and business succe

source_jobs.title: Field Marketing Lead
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

The financial advisory industry is a $80T market in the US, but has seen little technological innovation compared to the self-service market (Robinhood, Betterment, Wealthfront, SoFi). Client data management is a universally painful and expensive problem for financial advisors. Today, advisors manually collect, update, and utilize client data across the 7-10 software tools that they use to run their business. Data transcription and translation across systems is time-consuming and error-prone, leading to costly inefficiencies and embarrassing client-facing problems. Managing data access control, audit logs, and compliance is a constant challenge, particularly in larger organizations.

We are building the first wealth tech data orchestration company. We sync client data across a financial advisor's tech stack, unlocking the efficiency of a fully-integrated software suite with the choice of tools that best fit their business. We are laser-focused on enabling the best technology experiences for advisors and unlocking the potential of the entire wealth tech software ecosystem. We’re an experienced team of start-up operators with deep roots in both tech and advisory. You can learn more about us on our website https://www.oneadvisory.co/.



Role overview:

 - You own Dispatch's field marketing program end to end — conferences, executive dinners, roadshows, and regional events that put Dispatch in front of ICP companies and build pipeline.

 - This is our first dedicated field marketing hire. You own the function, not a slice of it.

 - You report to the VP of Marketing, take the direction we set, and turn it into in-market presence that executes flawlessly.

 - You partner closely with Sales to aim every program at C-suite enterprise buyers and tie it back to pipeline.

What you'll own:

 - Event Execution — You run industry conferences, executive dinners, roadshows, and custom events end to end: logistics, vendors, timelines, budgets, and on-site delivery.

 - Project Management — You keep dozens of moving pieces on track across overlapping programs, holding hard deadlines without dropping a detail.

 - Pipeline Partnership — You work hand in hand with Sales to design programs that reach C-suite enterprise buyers and convert presence into pipeline.

 - Performance & ROI — You measure every program against pipeline and spend, then report results and learnings back to marketing leadership.

 - Field Calendar — You sequence the field calendar with demand gen, brand, and the VP so priorities, budget, and timing line up.

Experience & Background:

 - 4–6 years in field or event marketing at B2B SaaS companies.

 - A track record marketing to C-suite enterprise buyers through long, multi-stakeholder sales cycles.

 - Hands-on ownership of events end to end — you've run the logistics, managed the budgets, and stood on the floor on event day, not just briefed an agency.

 - Experience at an early-stage startup — ideally Series B or earlier, or fewer than 100 people at time of joining.

 - Wealth management or financial advisory domain knowledge is not required. We'll teach you the industry.

Characteristics:

 - Relentless Executor — You bring urgency and edge to every program. When something breaks the week of an event, you stay calm, fix it, and keep everything else moving.

 - Master of the Craft — Event production and project management are your craft, not just your job. You've run enough programs to know where they go sideways and how to keep them on track.

 - Sharp Prioritizer — You make sound calls under ambiguity. When the calendar and budget can't fit everything, you decide what matters and what to cut, and you explain why.

 - Driven and Proactive — You don't wait to be handed a plan. You spot the gap, raise your hand, and own the outcome without being asked twice.

 - Low-Ego Partner — You earn trust with Sales, vendors, and teammates. You're easy to work with, quick to give credit, and quick to own a miss.

You'll st

source_jobs.title: Fullstack Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

The financial advisory industry is a $80T market in the US, but has seen little technological innovation compared to the self-service market (Robinhood, Betterment, Wealthfront, SoFi). Client data management is a universally painful and expensive problem for financial advisors. Today, advisors manually collect, update, and utilize client data across the 7-10 software tools that they use to run their business. Data transcription and translation across systems is time-consuming and error-prone, leading to costly inefficiencies and embarrassing client-facing problems. Managing data access control, audit logs, and compliance is a constant challenge, particularly in larger organizations.

We are building the first wealth tech data orchestration company. We sync client data across a financial advisor's tech stack, unlocking the efficiency of a fully-integrated software suite with the choice of tools that best fit their business. We are laser-focused on enabling the best technology experiences for advisors and unlocking the potential of the entire wealth tech software ecosystem. We’re an experienced team of start-up operators with deep roots in both tech and advisory. You can learn more about us on our website https://www.oneadvisory.co/.




ROLE DETAILS


ROLE OVERVIEW:

We're looking for a fullstack engineer to build features across our platform. You'll own end-to-end delivery of product workflows, working across frontend interfaces and backend services to help financial advisors manage client data seamlessly across their tech stack.


WHERE YOU'LL MAKE AN IMPACT:

 - Build and ship features end-to-end, working across React, Node.js and Golang.

 - Develop user-facing workflows including data collection forms, reporting interfaces, and account management experiences that handle complex financial data.

 - Implement backend APIs and services that power our data syncing and orchestration capabilities, ensuring reliable data flow across systems.

 - Write well-tested, maintainable code and participate in code reviews, contributing to the team's technical quality and standards.

 - Collaborate with Product, Design, and other engineers to break down requirements, identify technical approaches, and deliver solutions efficiently.

 - Debug and resolve production issues across the stack, ensuring system reliability for our customers.


REQUIREMENTS:

 - 4-6 years of fullstack engineering experience with exposure to both frontend and backend development.

 - Solid fundamentals in web development, including JavaScript/TypeScript, modern frontend frameworks (React preferred), API design, and working with databases.

 - Comfortable working across the full stack - you may have a preference but can contribute effectively on both frontend and backend work.

 - Collaborative mindset and strong communication skills - you work effectively with teammates and ask good questions when you need clarity.

 - Eager to learn and grow your technical skills - you're curious about new technologies and open to feedback.

 - Self-directed and capable of owning features with minimal oversight, while knowing when to ask for input or guidance.

 - Bias toward shipping and iteration - you balance getting things done with building things well.


YOU'LL STAND OUT IF YOU HAVE:

 - Experience building B2B SaaS products or working in fintech/financial services.

 - Exposure to building integrations, working with third-party APIs, or data transformation pipelines.

 - Background at startups where you had to wear multiple hats and ship features independently.

 - Experience with testing frameworks and writing automated tests for both frontend and backend code.

 - Interest in using AI tools to improve your development workflow and productivity.

Our tech stack: React, TypeScript, Node.js, Fastify, Golang, PostgreSQL

Why work at Dispatch?

 - We’re a highly experienced team with a track record of successfully building together. We’re well-verse

source_jobs.title: Head of Solutions
source_jobs.description:
Role overview:

 - Lead Solutions Engineering at Dispatch — the technical function responsible for pre-sales solutioning, scoping, and driving the most technical parts of implementation (field mapping and permissions) from first discovery call through go-live.

 - Serve as our most senior Solutions Engineer — personally leading solutioning on our most complex and strategic deals (a role currently held by the co-founder).

 - Collaborate with the Head of CS and Head of Sales to define and refine the end-to-end process from deal pursuit through implementation — ensuring clean handoffs and a consistent customer experience.

 - Work closely with Product and Engineering to surface insights from prospects and customers — translating field learnings into product direction.

 - Accountable for three outcomes: deals move faster through technical discovery; implementations complete on time and to scope; integration misalignments surface in pre-sales, not mid-implementation.



What you'll own:

 - Team & Pipeline Coverage — Build and develop a Solutions team capable of handling 5–8 concurrent enterprise deals, ensuring every opportunity has rigorous technical coverage and no deal goes in undersolutioned.

 - Internal Operating System — Systematize the solutioning process into a repeatable, well-documented playbook; design the team cadences — deal reviews, pipeline coverage checks, solutioning quality reviews — that drive consistency and keep standards high.

 - Cross-Functional Operating Model — Define the handoffs, artifacts, and rhythms between Solutions, Sales, CS, and Engineering that eliminate implementation surprises and accelerate time to value.

 - Product Expertise — Build a culture of deep product knowledge within the Solutions team — establishing the rhythms that keep the team sharp as the product evolves.

 - Enablement & Documentation — Build and maintain the artifacts that make Solutions knowledge scalable and transferable: solutioning playbooks, discovery frameworks, integration guides, and AE-facing materials.



Experience & Background:

 - 10+ years in B2B SaaS — specifically at a company that went through hyper-scale and serves a significant Enterprise customer segment.
   
   - 3+ years as an individual contributor Solutions Engineer
   
   - 5+ years managing a team of Solutions Engineers

 - Experience at an early-stage startup — ideally Series B or earlier, or fewer than 100 people at time of joining.

 - Proven track record of personally serving as the senior Solutions lead on complex, multi-stakeholder enterprise deals — from first technical conversation through signed contract.

 - Experience building or significantly scaling a Solutions function from the ground up — establishing process, team design, hiring, and cross-functional operating model where little existed before.



Characteristics:

 - Player-Builder — equally comfortable running a live solutioning session with a prospect's CTO and designing your team's operating model. You don't delegate the work you haven't mastered yourself.

 - Solution Designer, Not A Product Demonstrator — you understand what a customer wants to achieve, their constraints, and how to configure a flexible platform to get them there. You sell outcomes, not features.

 - Systems Builder — you naturally create repeatable frameworks, artifacts, and playbooks out of ambiguous, ad hoc situations. You leave every function more systematized than you found it.

 - Strong Client-Facing Presence — you command a room with senior technical buyers, hold your own in deep technical conversations at the CTO level, adapt your communication style across Sales, CS, Engineering, and the C-suite, and translate complex requirements into crisp documentation everyone can act on.

 - People Developer — invested in coaching and developing Solutions Engineers, creating a culture of product expertise, accountability, and high standards.



You'll stand out if you have:

 - Hands-on technical proficiency —

notes: Dispatch is a financial advisory industry-focused company with a strong engineering core, as evidenced by the high proportion of tech roles and the presence of senior engineering leadership positions.
